Project: AI Decision Logging Specification

Version: 0.1.0

Description:
Open specification defining tamper-evident AI decision logs and audit trail exports.
Provides a standardized format for recording and verifying AI system decisions
for governance, auditing, and regulatory compliance.

Specification location:
https://github.com/certifieddata/ai-decision-logging-spec

Reference implementation and maintainer:
https://certifieddata.io

---

Purpose:
AI systems operating in high-risk domains must maintain auditable logs of their decisions.
This specification defines how to:
  - Structure individual decision records (required and optional fields)
  - Chain records with SHA-256 hashes to ensure tamper-evidence
  - Sign records and exports with Ed25519 for cryptographic integrity
  - Export audit trail bundles for regulatory submission or third-party audit

---

Key concepts:
- decision records: structured JSON objects capturing model version, input reference, output, confidence, and reasoning
- hash chaining: SHA-256 hash of each record includes the hash of the prior record, creating a tamper-evident chain
- chain integrity anchors: periodic signed checkpoints capturing the chain tail hash and record count; enable partial verification without replaying the full chain
- Ed25519 signatures: cryptographic signatures over canonical (RFC 8785 JCS) JSON bodies; signing key published at well-known URL
- audit trail exports: time-bounded collections of decision records with chain integrity metadata
- artifact certificate linkage: decision records may reference certified AI artifacts via certificate ID
- redacted records: PII-redacted records that preserve chain integrity for public or regulatory export; carry sterilized: true as a machine-readable flag

---

CertifiedData implementation mapping:
  decision record         → decision_records (id, record_hash, previous_hash, canonical_payload, signature, key_id)
  public-safe record      → public_decision_log (public_payload, public_hash, previous_public_hash, signature, key_id)
  chain integrity anchor  → log_checkpoints (latest_record_hash, record_count, checkpoint_hash, signature)
  artifact certificate    → certificates (payload, signature, signing_key_id, retention_until)
  transparency log        → certificate_transparency_log (hash-chained append-only issuance log)
  artifact registration   → artifact_registrations (artifact_hash, trust_level, provenance, claims)
  audit vault             → audit_vault_records (signed archival; 7-year retention_until)
  signing keys            → https://certifieddata.io/.well-known/signing-keys.json (live; all active + rotated Ed25519 keys; key_id, alg, public_key_pem, public_key_raw_b64url)

---

Conformance levels:
  Level 1 (Core): required fields, hash chaining, record integrity
  Level 2 (Full): Level 1 + Ed25519 signatures, audit trail export, artifact certificate linkage

---

Regulatory alignment:
  EU AI Act Article 12: automatic logging of events for high-risk AI systems
  EU AI Act Article 19: retention of logs; minimum period varies by sector and national law
  NIST AI RMF: supports GOVERN, MAP, MEASURE, and MANAGE functions

Retention note:
  The specification floor is 6 months (EU AI Act Article 19 general minimum).
  The CertifiedData reference implementation retains decision records, certificates,
  and audit vault records for 7 years by default, consistent with financial services
  obligations. Healthcare implementations may require up to 15 years.

---

Schemas:
  schemas/decision-record.schema.json
  schemas/audit-trail.schema.json

Examples:
  examples/decision-record.example.json
  examples/audit-trail-export.example.json
  examples/verify-chain.js

  SPEC.md

Implementation guide:
